response.original_title: Opekkha a Film by Partho Paulinews Folia
response.overview: Opekkha follows Tahmida Jannat, a young girl diagnosed with cancer, whose final days are shaped by love, loss, and reflection. Her deepest bond is with her younger sister Sarin, who stands by her despite their broken family. As she battles illness, Tahmida writes a diary capturing her pain, memories, and longing. Her boyfriend Sunny abandons her after her diagnosis, choosing her friend Moumita instead. In contrast, Risan enters her life and loves her unconditionally, offering comfort through long conversations. After her death, Tahmida sends her diary to Risan, who learns the depth of her emotions through her words. The story unfolds as a moving monologue, highlighting how people live in hardship while others stay. In her final thoughts, she expresses how much she will miss her sister, her friends, her school, her family, and the simple joys of life.
response.title: Opekkha a Film by Partho Paulinews Folia
